docs.title:
Documentation

Welcome to the documentation hub for . All technical documentation lives here — architecture decisions, feature guides, and operational runbooks.
Sections
docs.text: Section Description ADRs Architecture Decision Records — why we made the choices we did Features Feature-level documentation and usage guides

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
For contributors: To run the docs site locally: 

pip install -r requirements.txt
mkdocs serve
Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Architecture Decision Records
docs.text: ADRs document significant technical decisions — what we chose, why, and what we considered. Each ADR is immutable once accepted; superseded decisions link forward to their replacement.

docs.title: Log
docs.text: # Title Status Date 0001 Example Decision Accepted YYYY-MM-DD

docs.title: Status Key
docs.text:
• Proposed — under discussion
  • Accepted — approved and in effect
  • Deprecated — no longer recommended
  • Superseded — replaced by a newer ADR (link provided in the record)

docs.title: Creating a New ADR
docs.text:
1. Copy 0001-example-decision.md and increment the number.
  2. Fill in the template sections.
  3. Set status to Proposed and open a PR for team review.
  4. Update this index table when merged.

docs.title: 0001 - Example Decision
docs.text: Field Value Status Accepted Date YYYY-MM-DD Deciders @handle1, @handle2 Supersedes — Superseded by —

docs.title: Context
docs.text: We needed to decide because .

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: We will use because .

docs.title: Alternatives Considered
docs.text: Option Pros Cons Option A ... ... Option B ... ...

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text:
Positive:

  • Negative / Trade-offs:

    • Neutral:

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature documentation covers what each feature does, how to use it, and how it is configured. Each page corresponds to a discrete feature or capability.

docs.title: Index
docs.text: Feature Description Status Example Feature Placeholder feature description Active

docs.title: Adding a Feature Doc
docs.text:
1. Copy example-feature.md and rename it to match the feature slug.
  2. Fill in the template sections.
  3. Add a row to the index table above.

docs.title: Example Feature
docs.text: Status: Active Owner: @handle Related ADR: 0001 - Example Decision

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: allows to so that .

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Example command or API call
curl -X POST https://api.example.com/v1/feature \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -d '{"key": "value"}'

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description FEATURE_ENABLED bool true Enables/disables the feature FEATURE_TIMEOUT_MS int 5000 Request timeout in milliseconds

docs.title: POST /v1/feature
docs.text:
Request body: 

{
  "key": "string"
}

Response: 

{
  "id": "string",
  "status": "string"
}
